# UC-18.3.20 · Fabric Host Route and ARP Scale Headroom

## Description

EVPN Type-2 host routes and ARP scale drive TCAM and forwarding table use on leafs. Running out of headroom stalls new workload placement and causes black-holed traffic during scale-out events. Trending utilization against hardware limits informs purchase timing and route summarization design.

## Value

EVPN Type-2 host routes and ARP scale drive TCAM and forwarding table use on leafs. Running out of headroom stalls new workload placement and causes black-holed traffic during scale-out events. Trending utilization against hardware limits informs purchase timing and route summarization design.

## Implementation

(1) Poll `show system internal forwarding resource` or vendor equivalents nightly plus hourly if above 75%. (2) Map limits per platform SKU via lookup. (3) Join with EVPN route summary growth (UC-18.3.10).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: NX-API/CLI scripted input, SNMP.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype="fabric:route_scale"` with fields `host`, `host_routes`, `host_route_limit`, `arp_entries`, `arp_limit`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll `show system internal forwarding resource` or vendor equivalents nightly plus hourly if above 75%. (2) Map limits per platform SKU via lookup. (3) Join with EVPN route summary growth (UC-18.3.10).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="fabric:route_scale" earliest=-24h
| eval host_pct=round(100*host_routes/host_route_limit,2)
| eval arp_pct=round(100*arp_entries/arp_limit,2)
| where host_pct>80 OR arp_pct>80
| stats latest(host_pct) as host_pct latest(arp_pct) as arp_pct latest(host_routes) as hr latest(arp_entries) as arp by host
| table host, host_pct, arp_pct, hr, arp
| sort -host_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fabric Host Route and ARP Scale Headroom** — EVPN Type-2 host routes and ARP scale drive TCAM and forwarding table use on leafs. Running out of headroom stalls new workload placement and causes black-holed traffic during scale-out events. Trending utilization against hardware limits informs purchase timing and route summarization design.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype="fabric:route_scale"` with fields `host`, `host_routes`, `host_route_limit`, `arp_entries`, `arp_limit`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): NX-API/CLI scripted input, SNMP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: fabric:route_scale. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="fabric:route_scale", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **host_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **arp_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where host_pct>80 OR arp_pct>80` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **Fabric Host Route and ARP Scale Headroom**): table host, host_pct, arp_pct, hr, arp
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (headroom %), Table (critical leafs), Timechart (host route growth).

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="fabric:route_scale" earliest=-24h
| eval host_pct=round(100*host_routes/host_route_limit,2)
| eval arp_pct=round(100*arp_entries/arp_limit,2)
| where host_pct>80 OR arp_pct>80
| stats latest(host_pct) as host_pct latest(arp_pct) as arp_pct latest(host_routes) as hr latest(arp_entries) as arp by host
| table host, host_pct, arp_pct, hr, arp
| sort -host_pct
```

## Visualization

Gauge (headroom %), Table (critical leafs), Timechart (host route growth).
